audience_and_territories: DataHub Agent Hackathon judges and Devpost visitors worldwide
Korea and United States platform audiences

creative_intent.purpose: Make ML release readiness legible by reading DataHub lineage, blocking incomplete evidence, and recording a deterministic decision receipt.
creative_intent.audience: Data and ML platform engineers evaluating production release evidence.
creative_intent.voice_or_art_direction: Graphite-and-cobalt evidence console with a compact three-column lineage-to-decision hierarchy.
deliberate_distinctive_choices: The product surface centers a four-node lineage chain and a right-rail REPAIR verdict instead of a generic dashboard grid.
Every decision shows the exact URN, evidence gap, stable digest, and DataHub write-back property.
The demo fixture is synthetic and intentionally exposes three repair gaps rather than claiming a false approval.
directions_explored: Generated a visual concept for a DataHub lineage evidence console, then implemented the information architecture in product code.
Selected a deterministic REPAIR-first demo state so the judge can see the safety boundary in one pass.

tools_or_models: Codex GPT-5.6 as main orchestrator and final validator
OpenAI image generation for the internal visual concept reference
generation_or_edit_stages: Browser runtime readback and source audit

human_accountability.compatibility_note: Responsible editor is the authorized agent final validator; no exact external rule currently requires a separate human actor for this code submission.
human_accountability.responsible_editor: Codex (authorized agent final validator) on behalf of the owner
human_accountability.external_human_action_basis: No human-only action identified in the current DataHub hackathon rules for a code/UI entry.
material_edits_or_acceptance_rationale: Replaced static mock evidence with a real local DataHub SDK round-trip fixture.
Kept REPAIR as the visible verdict because owner, freshness, and rollback evidence are intentionally absent.
Added full Apache-2.0 license text and removed credentials from repository content.
final_readback_evidence: Browser CDP runtime at http://127.0.0.1:4194 with zero page errors

disclosure_assessment.actor_role: Ordinary AI-assisted tool user and editor submitting an open-source code/UI project.
jurisdictions_checked: EU baseline for future public exposure
contract_or_client_requirements: DataHub hackathon requires public Apache-2.0 source and a demo video; no additional AI-label requirement was found in the current rules.
disclosure_assessment.material_deception_risk: Low: the artifact is an explicitly synthetic code fixture and does not depict a real person, event, endorsement, or production release approval.
disclosure_assessment.decision_rationale: The public artifact is code/UI with a synthetic DataHub fixture, not realistic synthetic media or a materially misleading claim. Internal AI provenance is retained in this record and README evidence boundaries remain explicit.
disclosure_assessment.public_label_or_control_applied: No extra public AI label; Apache-2.0 license and truthful synthetic-fixture disclosure remain visible.

medium_specific_checklist: Product-specific lineage-to-decision hierarchy verified in the rendered UI.
No placeholder data in the proof fixture; all nodes carry DataHub URNs.
Deterministic JS and Python receipts match digest LR-2842C6.
Full Apache-2.0 text present; no token, password, or private-key material found in source.
render_playback_device_or_runtime_eviden: npm run build: Vite production build passed
Browser CDP viewport 1920x911, button interaction verified, page_errors=[]
DataHub SDK round-trip readback: REPAIR / LR-2842C6 and lineage_receipt_verdict=REPAIR
independent_review_evidence: Codex final-validator review against current DataHub hackathon rules and AI-output release policy
